title: Aureline named-workset artifact, scope-truth chip, and scope-widen diff record
description: Seed schema for the durable, portable named-workset / sparse-scope artifact, its scope-truth chip projection, and its widen/narrow diff record. The durable artifact here is the stored, nameable, portable thing a user or admin curates; ADR-0009's schemas/runtime/execution_context.schema.json workset_scope union is the runtime projection that activated surfaces (search, graph, AI, task) read. This schema does not redefine the runtime scope-class vocabulary; it freezes the stable artifact shape, the chip contract every open/search/support/AI/refactor/export surface renders, and the typed diff record that powers scope-widening review. Raw absolute paths, credential material, and the exact membership list of a policy-limited hidden set never appear. Include/exclude patterns are carried verbatim (they are user-authored) but the schema keeps a redaction-aware budget for presentation labels and free-text notes. Adding a new source class, chip class, narrowing cause, portability class, or diff-class is additive-minor and bumps workset_artifact_schema_version; repurposing an existing enum value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

workset_artifact_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the workset-artifact payload shape. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape.

opaque_id.description: Opaque stable identifier safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests, and safe in support bundles. Raw absolute paths, credentials, and authority tokens never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: Producer-local monotonic timestamp. Opaque to this schema; exporters may use any monotonic or pseudo-monotonic source.

redaction_aware_text.description: Redaction-aware free text (<= 1024 graphemes). Exporters apply redaction policy before the value crosses the schema boundary.

fixture_metadata.description: Optional worked-example prelude carried on fixtures.

instance_schema_hint.description: Optional schema hint carried on worked fixtures.

scope_class.description: Frozen scope-class vocabulary used by the artifact, the chip, and the diff record. Intentionally narrower than ADR-0009's runtime workset_scope union: this artifact freeze covers only the five classes the open/search foundations render on a chip. A review workspace or companion surface activates through execution-context's runtime projection, not through this artifact.

membership_policy.description: How the artifact enumerates its members. explicit_root_list and glob_pattern are user-authored; dependency_graph_reachable is derived from a graph seed; manifest_driven quotes an external workset manifest (e.g., VCS-committed workset file) by reference.

source_class.description: Where the artifact lives. local_only = this machine's profile; workspace_shared = committed/shared with the workspace; profile_imported = pulled from a sync/portable-state package; managed = owned by a managed workspace provider; ephemeral_session = current session only, not persisted.

portability_class.description: How the artifact travels. fully_portable = identity and member refs survive export/import; portable_with_rebinding = survives export but member refs must re-resolve on import; machine_local_only = refs to machine-local paths/devices that will not rebind cleanly; managed_provider_locked = the artifact is owned by a managed provider and cannot be exported.

readiness_state.description: Index/materialization readiness for the artifact's members, using the same vocabulary as the UI/UX spec Appendix AC workset switcher row.

partial_truth_label.description: Re-export of the TAD 12.6 partial-truth labels. Describes how much of one member ref is actually materialized.

narrowing_cause.description: Why members are hidden from a policy-limited view. admin_policy and license_or_export_control are the only classes that never expose the hidden list outside a policy-admin surface; the rest expose counts and may expose member kinds.

diff_class.description: Typed class for one diff entry between two artifact revisions or between an artifact and a candidate widening. identity_change means workset_id changed (forbidden under diff semantics — see rules).

filesystem_root_ref.description: Opaque reference to an ADR-0006 filesystem-identity record. Raw absolute paths, canonical filesystem objects, or save-target tokens are never inlined here; consumers resolve the ref through the filesystem-identity surface.

ref_kind.description: Kind of member reference. root carries a filesystem_root_ref; folder and module carry a workspace-scoped logical ref; manifest_entry quotes a row from an external manifest; graph_seed is the anchor used by dependency_graph_reachable membership.

ref.description: Stable opaque ref. For root kind, this is an opaque pointer to an ADR-0006 filesystem-identity record (same shape as filesystem_root_ref). For folder/module/manifest_entry/graph_seed, this is a workspace-scoped opaque id.

member_ref.description: One member reference inside the artifact. Counts and class labels flow across every boundary; raw absolute paths never do.

pattern_kind.description: Whether the row includes or excludes.

pattern.description: Literal pattern text as authored by the user. Globs, workspace-relative paths, or module selectors are carried verbatim. Raw absolute paths are discouraged and should be collapsed to workspace-relative form before crossing the schema boundary.

applies_to_root_ref.description: Optional root scoping. Null means the pattern applies across every root in root_refs.

pattern_entry.description: One include/exclude row. Order of rows is stable; later rows never silently override earlier rows — conflicts are surfaced as narrowing/widening causes on the diff record.

underlying_workset_ref.description: Pointer to the underlying pre-narrowing workset_artifact_record or runtime scope descriptor.

policy_ref.description: Opaque reference to the policy that narrowed the view. Raw policy bodies never appear here.

hidden_member_count.description: Count of members hidden by the policy. The exact list is only exposed through hidden_member_list_visible = true, which is forbidden for admin_policy and license_or_export_control causes.

hidden_member_list_visible.description: True only when narrowing_cause permits the hidden list to project outside a policy-admin surface. admin_policy and license_or_export_control MUST set this to false.

policy_limitation_block.description: Policy-limited narrowing block. Attached only when scope_class = policy_limited_view.

profile_sync_group_ref.description: Optional profile sync group the artifact belongs to when source_class = profile_imported or workspace_shared.

portability_metadata.description: Portability metadata that lets export, import, and support flows reason about whether the artifact will survive a round-trip and what will need to rebind.

hidden_result_count_known.description: Whether the producer can emit an exact hidden-result count for this artifact. False means a chip may say 'results may be hidden' without naming a number.

hidden_result_count.description: Exact count of hidden members/results when known. Omitted or null when hidden_result_count_known = false.

readiness_metadata.description: Readiness and hidden-result accounting attached to the artifact.

workset_id.description: Stable workset identity. Never reused after removal. Every downstream chip, diff, search packet, support bundle, and AI/refactor/export record references this id.

presentation_subtitle.description: Optional subtitle such as 'Payments backend hot path'.

workspace_ref.description: Owning workspace reference. Null only when scope_class = current_repo and the artifact is a one-root fallback shape that predates an explicit workspace.

root_refs.description: Filesystem roots in scope for this artifact. Multi-root artifacts list every root here; cross-repo result grouping reads this list to label each result group.

patterns.description: User-authored include/exclude patterns. Order is stable. Empty array is allowed for scope_class = full_workspace and current_repo when no refinement is applied.

member_refs.description: Resolved member refs after patterns and membership_policy are applied. Empty only for ephemeral / warming artifacts whose policy is manifest_driven or dependency_graph_reachable and has not resolved yet.

policy_limitation.description: Policy-limited narrowing block. Required when scope_class = policy_limited_view; forbidden otherwise.

parent_workset_ref.description: Optional parent workset when this artifact was derived by narrowing or widening an existing artifact. Diff records connect parent and child.

manifest_source_ref.description: Optional reference to an external workset manifest file when membership_policy = manifest_driven.

workset_artifact_record.description: Durable, portable, nameable workset artifact. One artifact per stable id. Surfaces (open, search, support, AI, refactor, export) reference this record by workset_id; none of them re-mint a parallel stable id for the same scope.

chip_presentation_state.description: Presentation state a scope-truth chip renders in. active_narrow_safe = narrow boundary is the current scope and no hidden content is relevant; active_partial = scope is active but not fully indexed; active_policy_limited = scope is active but policy hides members; active_widened = scope was widened beyond the declared workset; outside_current_scope = the chip marks a result that sits outside the current scope.

chip_surface_class.description: Which surface the chip renders on. This list is the deliberate seed surface family; each surface reads the same chip contract instead of inventing a surface-local scope label.

chip_action.description: Typed chip action the shell may offer on the chip. reveal_hidden_results_policy_admin_only is only rendered when policy_limitation.hidden_member_list_visible = true and the surface is a policy-admin surface.

known.description: Whether a meaningful hidden-result count is known. False means the chip renders a shape like 'some results may be hidden' without a number.

count_class.description: Typed class of hidden-result summary. outside_scope_roots is what a search surface reports when results exist in other roots but are excluded by the active workset.

hidden_result_summary.description: Summary the chip renders when hidden or excluded results are relevant.

workset_ref.description: Reference to the workset_artifact_record the chip projects. A chip NEVER invents a stable scope id; it quotes workset_ref.

chip_label.description: User-visible chip label. Resolves from the closed label family { 'Current repo', 'Selected workset', 'Full workspace', 'Sparse slice', 'Policy-limited view', 'Outside current scope' } plus the workset name where relevant. Final copy lives in the shell interaction-safety contract; this field carries the resolved string.

root_count.description: Number of roots the active scope covers. >= 2 means the chip is a multi-root chip; = 1 means single-root; null is reserved for ephemeral / warming chips where the count is not known yet.

outside_current_scope_marker_visible.description: True when the chip marks a specific result as sitting outside the current scope (cross-repo result row, outside-scope preview, support packet excerpt). False otherwise.

scope_truth_chip_record.description: One scope-truth chip projection. Every open-flow trust card, search-result group header, cross-repo result group, support-packet header, AI context inspector, refactor scope footer, and export scope footer reads this shape. A surface that renders a scope label without a backing chip record is non-conforming.

diff_entry.description: One typed diff entry. diff_class = identity_change is forbidden on any diff where both base and candidate exist; identity never drifts under a diff.

widens_scope.description: True if the candidate adds at least one member, broadens at least one pattern, or widens the policy envelope.

narrows_scope.description: True if the candidate removes at least one member, narrows at least one pattern, or narrows the policy envelope.

presentation_only.description: True when every diff entry is of diff_class = presentation_only (workset_name change, presentation_subtitle change). A presentation-only diff MUST NOT set widens_scope, narrows_scope, changes_portability, or changes_readiness.

expected_index_cost_class.description: Rough index/fetch cost class for materializing the candidate. Consumers render this on the scope-diff review sheet without claiming precise timings.

scope_widen_diff_record.description: Typed diff between two workset artifacts (or between an active workset and a candidate widening). Powers the scope-diff review sheet called out in the UI/UX spec Appendix AC.4. Widening scope is always an explicit action; this record is the evidence for that action.
